back_translation: The angel of the LORD went up from Gilgal to Bochim and said, 'I brought you up from Egypt and led you into the land that I had sworn to your fathers, and I said, I will not break my covenant with you forever.'
'And you shall not make a covenant with the inhabitants of this land — you shall tear down their altars.' But you did not obey my voice. What is this you have done?
So I also said, 'I will not drive them out from before you any longer; they will be thorns in your sides, and their gods will be a snare to you.'
When the angel of the LORD spoke these words to all the descendants of Israel, the people lifted up their voices and wept loudly.
They called the name of that place Bochim, and offered sacrifices there to the LORD.
After Joshua dismissed the people, the descendants of Israel went out to take possession of the land, each to his own inheritance.
The people served the LORD throughout the days of Joshua, and throughout the days of the elders who outlived Joshua, who had seen all the great works that the LORD had done for Israel.
Then Joshua son of Nun, the servant of the LORD, died at the age of 110 years.
They buried him in the territory of his inheritance, at Timnath-heres in the hill country of Ephraim, north of Mount Gaash.
When that whole generation was also gathered to their fathers, and another generation rose up after them, who did not know the LORD or the works that He had done for Israel.
The descendants of Israel did evil in the sight of the LORD and served the Baals.

They forsook the LORD, the God of their fathers, who had brought them out of the land of Egypt, and went after other gods — the gods of the peoples around them — and bowed down to them, provoking the LORD to anger.
They forsook the LORD and served Baal and the Ashtoreths.

The anger of the LORD burned against Israel, and He delivered them into the hand of plunderers who plundered them, and sold them into the hand of their enemies around them. They could no longer stand against their enemies.
In every place they went out, the hand of the LORD was against them to bring calamity, just as the LORD had said and as the LORD had sworn to them. They were in great distress.
Then the LORD raised up judges, who saved them from the hand of those who plundered them.

But they did not obey their judges. Instead they prostituted themselves with other gods and bowed down to them. They quickly turned from the way their fathers had walked, who obeyed the commandments of the LORD — they did not do as their fathers had done.

When the LORD raised up judges for them, the LORD was with the judge and saved them from the hand of their enemies all the days of the judge, because the LORD had compassion on their groaning because of those who oppressed and afflicted them.

But when the judge died, they turned and acted more corruptly than their fathers, going after other gods to serve them and bow down to them. They did not give up their deeds and their stubborn ways at all.
The anger of the LORD burned against Israel, and He said, 'Because this nation has transgressed my covenant which I commanded their fathers, and has not obeyed my voice,
I will no longer drive out any nation from before them — of the nations that Joshua left when he died,
in order to test Israel by them — whether they will keep the way of the LORD by walking in it, as their fathers kept it.'
So the LORD let those nations remain, not driving them out quickly, and He did not deliver them into the hand of Joshua.
